title: Asking on the bus
desc: Make sure you're going the right way
setting: You've boarded a bus but aren't sure it goes where you need. You ask a fellow passenger.

options.es: Entschuldigung, fahrt de Bus zum Bahnhof?
options.en: Excuse me, does this bus go to the station?
options.feedback: Clear, polite, and exactly the question you need. Well done.
It might work with a questioning tone, but the full question is clearer.
A bit alarming! Ask specifically whether the bus goes where you want.

steps.speaker: Passagier
steps.es: Jaja, de gaht zum Bahnhof, no drü Halt.
steps.en: Yes, it goes to the station, three more stops.

options.en: Oh great, thank you very much!

options.feedback: Repeating it back shows you understood. Nice.
No need to doubt a helpful local. A simple thanks is enough.

steps.speaker: Passagier
steps.es: Ich säg Ihne denn, wänn Sie use mönd.
steps.en: I'll tell you when you need to get off.

options.es: Das isch sehr nett vo Ihne, merci!
options.en: That's very kind of you, thanks!
options.feedback: Lovely — acknowledging the kindness warmly is just right.
They're being kind — let them help. 'Merci' is warmer.

steps.speaker: Passagier
steps.es: So, jetz mönd Sie use. Da isch de Bahnhof!
steps.en: There, now you get off. Here's the station!

options.es: Merci viilmal für d'Hilf, schöne Tag!
options.en: Thanks a lot for the help, nice day!
options.feedback: Warm send-off honoring their help. You sound like a local.
Too flat after they watched out for your stop. Thank them properly.
